title: The Effect of High Dismissal Protection on Bureaucratic Turnover and Productivity
notes: EVENT STUDY REVIEWED. Estrada & Lombardi (2022) AEJ:Applied. Chile 2014 law reform granted permanent contracts to temp teachers with 3+ consecutive years. Treated: 3yr seniority, Control: 2yr seniority. Cohorts 2010-2014 (2014 is post). Simple DiD: reg y treated_post treated i.agno, cluster(clave). No unit FEs — treated is binary group indicator absorbed as FE. Outcome: left_school_plus1 (not working in same school after 1yr). Table 2, Col 1. Expected negative coefficient (dismissal protection reduces turnover). | HONESTDID STRUCTURAL NOTE (2026-04-19): D-FRAGILE classification is structural, NOT implementation failure. Paper has n_post=1 (only the 2014 post-reform year), so the HonestDiD Rambachan-Roth bound has no post-treatment variation to discipline — CIs at Mbar=0 mechanically include zero for all targets (first/avg/peak collapse to identical single-point estimate). Pre-trends are oscillating not drifting (no monotonic drift across 2010-2013). Sharp seniority-cutoff design + 4-estimator directional unanimity (TWFE/CS-NT/SA/Gardner all negative) provide supporting evidence. RCS aggregation gap (TWFE=-0.037 vs CS-NT=-0.030, 20%) is Pattern 25, within normal range. Paper-auditor: EXACT (0.49%).
